response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role demands deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in this resume. His closest touchpoints (low-latency DLang services, brief C/C++ IDE component work at National Instruments) are application-level software engineering, not silicon or accelerator performance architecture. The experience-years bar is met, but the domain mismatch is fundamental for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Long, consistent engineering track record (~15+ years) with staff/senior-level roles
Low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) handling millions of requests per second
Strong testing culture and open-source contributions, including authoring test frameworks and libraries
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No deep-learning workload, training/inference optimization, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture
